perturbation.original_text: Neither Participant nor its Representatives shall initiate any communications with any Representative of Target concerning the Evaluation Material without Originator's prior written consent.
perturbation.changed_text: Neither Participant nor its Representatives shall initiate any communications with any Representative of Target without Originator's consent.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'concerning the Evaluation Material' from the restriction on communications, the clause becomes overly broad. While the intent may have been to limit communication only about Evaluation Material, the removal creates in-text contradiction and uncertainty. Now Participant cannot initiate communication with Representative of Target without Originator's Consent, creating an in-text contradiction, because the meaning is not clear, as the communications about evaluation material are restricted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) Participant agrees that Participant will (i) use the Evaluation Material solely for the purposes of the Participation, (ii) not, directly or indirectly, use, <PAGE> disseminate, disclose, discuss or reveal any Evaluation Material, and (iii) otherwise treat and maintain in full confidence all Evaluation Material.
perturbation.justification: The original text restricts communication concerning 'Evaluation Material'. The changed text removes 'concerning the Evaluation Material', broadening the restriction to *any* communication with a Target representative without Originator's consent. This creates a contradiction with the contradicted text, which states the Participant agrees to (i) use the Evaluation Material solely for the purposes of the Participation, implying that the participant can communicate about matters *other* than evaluation material with the target representative without Originator's Consent. The modification now contradicts this implied allowance, creating a direct conflict and uncertainty about permissible communication, aligning with the 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that Participant or any Representative of Participant is requested or becomes legally compelled (by oral questions, interrogatories; requests for information or documents, subpoena, civil investigative demands or similar process) to disclose any of the Evaluation Material or take any other action prohibited by this Agreement, Participant shall, to the extent practicable, provide Originator with prompt written notice thereof (in reasonable detail) so that Originator may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that Participant or any Representative of Participant is requested or becomes legally compelled to disclose any of the Evaluation Material or take any other action prohibited by this Agreement, Participant shall, to the extent practicable, provide Originator with prompt written notice thereof so that Originator may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase '(by oral questions, interrogatories; requests for information or documents, subpoena, civil investigative demands or similar process)', the specific legal processes compelling disclosure are removed. This reduces the clarity of what 'legally compelled' entails. Removing it, creates in-text contradiction, creating ambiguity as to what legally compelling actions, participant should notify originator.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (f) In the event that Participant or any Representative of Participant is requested or becomes legally compelled to disclose any of the Evaluation Material or take any other action prohibited by this Agreement, Participant shall, to the extent practicable, provide Originator with prompt written notice thereof so that Originator may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy. In the event that such protective order or other remedy is not obtained, Participant shall furnish only that portion of the Evaluation Material or take only such action as is legally required by law or binding order.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the specific examples of legal compulsion, such as 'oral questions, interrogatories; requests for information or documents, subpoena, civil investigative demands or similar process'. This creates ambiguity regarding what constitutes 'legally compelled' disclosure. The contradicted text assumes a clear understanding of what 'legally compelled' means, and the modified text undermines this understanding, introducing a contradiction and making it unclear what specific actions trigger the notification requirement. Removing this phrase creates ambiguity as to what legally compelling actions, participant should notify originator, thus creating contradiction as defined by Omissions - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: To the extent the Evaluation Material includes or otherwise involves intellectual property, technical data, technology, processes, applications for patents, trade secrets or know-how, disclosure thereof to Participant or its Representatives by or on behalf of Originator, Target or any of their respective Representatives shall not be construed as the grant of any license or of any right to use the same, except for the sole purpose of evaluating and completing a potential Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: To the extent the Evaluation Material includes or otherwise involves intellectual property, technical data, technology, processes, applications for patents, trade secrets or know-how, disclosure thereof to Participant or its Representatives by or on behalf of Originator, Target or any of their respective Representatives shall not be construed as the grant of any license or of any right to use the same.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'except for the sole purpose of evaluating and completing a potential Transaction', the clause now seemingly prohibits *any* use of the IP, even for the intended purpose of the agreement. This omission creates an in-text contradiction, because the rest of the agreement hinges on Participants ability to evaluate for Transaction, but removing this part of sentence, creates a contradiction, making it impossible to evaluate.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) Participant agrees that Participant will (i) use the Evaluation Material solely for the purposes of the Participation, (ii) not, directly or indirectly, use, <PAGE> disseminate, disclose, discuss or reveal any Evaluation Material, and (iii) otherwise treat and maintain in full confidence all Evaluation Material.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed the participant to use the Evaluation Material for the purpose of evaluating and completing a potential Transaction. The changed text removes this exception, meaning the participant is not allowed to use the evaluation material for any purpose, including evaluation for transaction. The contradicted text states that the Participant will use the Evaluation Material solely for the purposes of the Participation, thus directly contradicting the Changed Text, because the Changed Text doesn't allow the participant to use the evaluation material. Therefore, this is a strong contradiction and should be classified as YES
